name: American Sign Language Teachers Association / NCIEC
source_note: Template hierarchy for teacher standards; refine against official ASLTA publications.

nodes.title: Language Knowledge and Performance
nodes.description: Domain 1: ASL proficiency and linguistic knowledge applied to teaching.

nodes.title: Demonstrates advanced ASL proficiency for instructional contexts
Applies knowledge of ASL linguistics to instruction and feedback
Models varied registers and discourse in ASL as appropriate to learners
Integrates Deaf cultural perspectives into language teaching
Instructional Planning and Delivery
nodes.description: Domain 2: Curriculum, lesson design, and teaching practice.

nodes.title: Aligns learning objectives, activities, and assessments
Uses evidence-based instructional strategies for ASL acquisition
Differentiates instruction to meet diverse learner needs
Uses technology and materials effectively in ASL instruction
Assessment and Professional Practice
nodes.description: Domain 3: Assessment literacy and professional responsibilities.

nodes.title: Designs and uses valid formative and summative assessments
Provides constructive feedback aligned to learning goals
Engages in reflection and continuous improvement
Upholds ethical and professional obligations to learners and field
